activities_projects.title: Human Capital Observation
activities_projects.objective: Understand how education and skill transform a person into a resource
steps: Identify three professionals in your neighborhood (e.g., Doctor, Teacher, Tailor)
List the specific 'Skill' or 'Education' they required for their job
Explain how their work contributes to the society's income
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Defines 'People as Resource' through real-life examples
activities_projects.evaluation: Clear identification of skills and contributions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Tale of Sakal and Vilas
activities_projects.objective: Compare the impact of investment in human capital
steps: Read the stories of Sakal and Vilas from the textbook
Draw a 'Success Path' for Sakal (Education -> Skill -> Job)
Draw a 'Cycle of Poverty' for Vilas (No education -> Poor health -> Low-paid labor)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the long-term benefits of education and health
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate depiction of the two contrasting life paths (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Economic vs. Non-Economic Activities
activities_projects.objective: Distinguish between activities that add to national income and those that don't
steps: List 5 activities done by your family members at home
Classify them into 'Market Activities' (paid) and 'Non-Market Activities' (unpaid)
Note why household work by women is generally not counted in National Income
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Differentiates between market and non-market activities
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct classification of provided examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Health Check-up Chart
activities_projects.objective: Understand the importance of health as an asset
steps: List 3 ways a healthy worker is more productive than an unhealthy one
Identify local government health facilities (Dispensaries/Hospitals) in your area
Write why a firm would prefer a healthy employee over a frequently ill one
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates physical well-being to work efficiency
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical reasoning regarding health and productivity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Education Trends in India
activities_projects.objective: Observe the growth of literacy in India
steps: Look at the literacy rate table in the chapter
Draw a simple bar graph showing the literacy rate of India from 1951 to 2011
Identify if there is a gap between male and female literacy rates
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the bar graph and observations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Identifying Unemployment Types
steps: Describe a situation where 8 people are working on a farm that only needs 5 (Disguised)
Describe a situation where a worker finds no work during non-harvest months (Seasonal)
Label which one is common in rural areas
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the two main types of rural unemployment
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of the scenarios (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Urban Unemployment Awareness
steps: Define 'Educated Unemployment' based on the text
Interview an elder about why even graduates struggle to find jobs in cities
Suggest one vocational skill that can help a student get a job faster
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the mismatch between education and job requirements
activities_projects.evaluation: Relevant definition and practical suggestions (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Quality of Population Factors
activities_projects.objective: Learn what determines the quality of a country's population
steps: Create a 'Triangle of Growth' with 'Literacy Rate', 'Health', and 'Skills' at the corners
Write inside the triangle how these three factors lead to 'National Income' growth
Decorate with images of schools and hospitals
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Summarizes the key drivers of human resource development
activities_projects.evaluation: Creative and accurate representation of the concept (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan Mission
activities_projects.objective: Learn about government initiatives in education
steps: Find out the goal of 'Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan' from the chapter
Describe the 'Mid-day Meal' scheme and its purpose
Note how these schemes help in increasing the attendance of children
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes government efforts to improve literacy
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct explanation of the schemes (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Investment in Self
activities_projects.objective: Reflect on personal human capital development
steps: List two things you are learning in school that will help you earn in the future
Identify one hobby or skill (e.g., drawing, coding, sports) you want to develop
Write why your parents are 'investing' in your education
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects theoretical concepts to personal life
activities_projects.evaluation: Personal reflection and understanding of 'investment' (10 marks)
